Stub repo for the iolanta browser.

methods.name: ldflex.ldflex.SPARQLParseException.highlight_code
documentation: Define lines to highlight.

SPARQL query is invalid.

Error:

```
{self.error}
```

Query:
```sparql hl_lines="{self.highlight_code}"
{self.query}
```

classes.name: ldflex.ldflex.LDFlex

methods.name: ldflex.ldflex.LDFlex.query
documentation:
Run a SPARQL `SELECT`, `CONSTRUCT`, or `ASK` query.

Args:
    query_text: The SPARQL text;
    **kwargs: bind variables in the query to values if necessary. For
        example:

        ```python
        ldflex.query(
            'SELECT ?title WHERE { ?page rdfs:label ?title }',
            ?page=page_iri,
        )
        ```

Returns:
    Results of the query:

    - a graph for `CONSTRUCT`,
    - a list of dicts for `SELECT`,
    - or a boolean for `ASK`.

functions.name: iolanta.reformat_blank_nodes.reformat_blank_nodes
documentation:
Prepend prefix to every blank node.

JSON-LD flattening creates sequential identifiers in the form of _:bN
for every blank node. See https://www.w3.org/TR/json-ld11/

This means that subgraphs of the Octiron ConjunctiveGraph have clashing
blank node identifiers.

To avoid that, we prepend a prefix to every blank node identifier.

methods.name: iolanta.loaders.base.Loader.__call__
documentation:
Call the loader to retrieve the document in a PYLD friendly format.

Used to resolve remote contexts.

The type of `source` parameter is intentionally `str`: that's the only
thing which pyld can do.

Base class for loaders.

Loader receives a URL (or a path) to certain location. It is responsible for
reading data from that location and returning it as a stream of RDF quads.

Usually, depending on the data format, Loader leverages Parsers for that
purpose.
noinspection TaskProblemsInspection

classes.name: iolanta.facets.errors.PageNotFound

methods.name: iolanta.facets.errors.PageNotFound.possible_path
documentation: Guess on a possible path.

methods.name: iolanta.facets.errors.PageNotFound.possible_id
documentation: Guess on a possible $id.

Page not found by IRI: `{self.iri}`.

!!! error "Page not found by IRI: `{self.iri}`"
    Every page on your documentation site, just as any other entity described on
    it, has an IRI — a unique identifier similar to a URL. IRI can be generated
    in two alternative ways.

      1. If you wanted to use IRI generated automatically, then please confirm
         that in the `docs` directory there is a file under the following path:

         ```
         {self.possible_path}
         ```

      2. If you wanted to use IRI that you specified explicitly then confirm
         there is a Markdown file somewhere in `docs` directory which contains
         the following text in its header:

         ```markdown
         ---
         $id: {self.possible_id}
         ---
         ```

documentation:
Python facet not callable.

!!! error "Cannot import an object or cannot call it."

      - Import path: `{self.path}`
      - Object imported: `{self.facet}`

    The imported Python object is not a callable and thus cannot be used as
    a facet.

classes.name: iolanta.facets.textual_class.facets.Class

methods.name: iolanta.facets.textual_class.facets.Class.stream_instances
documentation:
Query and stream class instances lazily.

The operation of rendering an instance is not pure: it might cause us
to retrieve more data and load said data into the graph. That's because
we do multiple query attempts.

We have to stop if a subsequent attempt returns no results. That's why
we can't use `funcy.distinct()` or something similar.
